name: git-helper
description: Common git operations (status, log, diff, show, add-commit, stash, branch, checkout, checkout-file/restore, log-diff, log-status, config, root) in one step, with optional directory for add-commit; config accepts multiple key-value pairs; show action supports --grep, -A/-B/-C context, --head/--tail to filter output in one step, replacing git show + grep pipe pattern; diff supports --stat/--name-only/--cached flags; add-commit auto-configures git identity on failure; restore action reverts file(s) to last committed state using git checkout --, with no args reverts all files (replaces git checkout -- . pattern); log-status combines log + status in one step replacing separate calls; with --cd to change directory before running.

parameters.name: --cd=DIR, -C DIR
parameters.description: Change to directory before running git command (replaces cd + git pattern)

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Additional arguments: file path for diff/show/checkout-file/restore (omit for restore-all), commit message for add-commit, N for log/log-diff/log-status, key+value pairs for config, directory for add-commit, starting directory for root; for show action: --grep=PATTERN, -A=N, -B=N, -C=N, --head=N, --tail=N; for diff action: --stat, --name-only, --cached, --head=N, --tail=N

examples.expected: Lines matching 'func doSource' with 40 lines of after-context

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses git branch/status/log/diff/add-commit/checkout-file/restore calls that would otherwise be separate steps; add-commit auto-configures git identity on failure, avoiding the failed-commit-then-fix two-step pattern; restore with no args replaces git checkout -- . pattern; show --grep replaces git show + grep + head/tail pipe chain, saving 2+ steps per use and reducing observation size; config with multiple pairs saves 1+ step per use; log-status replaces separate log + status steps, saving 1 step per use; diff --stat replaces git diff --stat without needing a separate call; --cd replaces cd + git two-step pattern.
